metadata.description: Unified fix for three interconnected problems: agents on cheaper models receive too much context (skills/tools/rules loaded indiscriminately), wrong model assigned to task complexity, and DeepSeek-v4-pro ignores explicit path instructions causing silent write failures in isolated crons.
metadata.origin: Day 33 standup investigation + Telegram L-047 findings

childA.title: Progressive Disclosure Engine
childA.scope: Skills/tools loaded on-demand by task type, not indiscriminately. Context stripping before dispatch.

childB.title: 2-Pass Dispatch Architecture
childB.scope: Yoda discovery/diagnosis on deepseek-pro → precise execution atoms dispatched to specialists on cheaper models. Formalize in TQP with atom templates.

childC.title: DeepSeek ~ Path Fix
childC.scope: Immediate mitigation for silent write failures. Options: symlink ~/.openclaw → /Users/ainchorsangiefpl/.openclaw, pre-flight path validator, or model swap for affected crons.

acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: No agent loads more than 3 relevant skills per task (progressive disclosure)
AC-2: Yoda performs discovery atom breakdown before specialist dispatch (2-pass)
AC-3: All cron-generated files use absolute paths — zero ~ bugs across any model
AC-4: Right model assigned to right task from available cloud models
AC-5: Model-task fit matrix documented and enforced in TQP routing

deepseek-v4-pro:cloud.strengths: Complex orchestration, multi-step reasoning, code generation
deepseek-v4-pro:cloud.bestFor: Yoda discovery, Thrawn architecture, complex analysis

deepseek-v4-flash:cloud.weaknesses: Less capable than pro on complex reasoning

kimi-k2.6:cloud.strengths: Coding-driven design, autonomous execution, swarm orchestration

gemma4:31b-cloud.weaknesses: Fails at multi-step discovery (L-047), slow cold-load

glm-5.1:cloud.potential: Coding-focused atoms, strong instruction adherence (needs evaluation)

nemotron-3-super:cloud.potential: Cost-effective specialist for complex atoms (needs evaluation)

metadata.relatedDecisions: DEC pending — Model-Task Fit Matrix approval

acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: No agent loads more than 3 relevant skills per task (Progressive Disclosure)
AC-2: Yoda performs discovery atom breakdown before specialist dispatch (2-Pass Dispatch)
AC-3: All cron-generated files use absolute paths — zero ~ bugs across any model (Path Safety)
AC-4: Model-Task la Fit Matrix implemented: Right model assigned to right task based on Ollama Cloud library capabilities
AC-5: TQP routing enforced based on Model-Task Fit Matrix

AC-6: TQP Trinity Implemented: (1) Tactical: Gatekeeper Tool enforces PG persistence before tool execution, (2) Structural: 2-Pass Dispatch eliminates instruction decay, (3) Architectural: Progressive Disclosure optimizes prompt context
metadata.tacticalFix: Option B: The Gatekeeper Tool — replace standard write/exec tools with wrappers that require a TQP index to run, forcing persistence as a prerequisite for execution.

title: Monthly Model Review Remediation — Benchmarks, Budget, Violations Audit
type:
Monthly model review (May 2026) surfaced 4 action items:
1. Run fresh benchmarks (gemma4:26b completed 7/8 PASS 87.5%, T06 structured JSON failed)
2. Fix drift violation (incident-log.json missing — ITIL-1 breach, RESOLVED)
3. Create violations audit log (model-drift-violations.json — RESOLVED)
4. Recalibrate budget cap from A$500/mo Claude-era to A$150/mo Ollama Cloud reality (A$100 Ollama fixed + A$50 Claude buffer) — PENDING KEN APPROVAL

Also: gemma4-benchmark.sh had a syntax bug on line 74 (dangling fi) — FIXED.

Ken approval needed: monthly budget cap recalibration to A$150/mo effective June 2026.

description:
L-045: Agent models systematically ignore absolute-path warnings in isolated cron sessions. 6 crons affected across 2 failure modes.

FAILURE MODE 1 — Tilde-Path (patched 2026-05-29):
- Morning Stand-Up (3c279099): ~ path — 22 days stale
- Daily Blog (a027fd60): timeout 600s + path risk — bumped to 900s
- Aria ROI (7a4d8381): ~ path in workspace-business

FAILURE MODE 2 — Timeout (folded into this ticket):
- PG Sync Check (f7668f6a): 4 consecutive 30s timeouts — too short for PG queries
- Monthly Model Strategy Review (38d77d14): 300s timeout — complex multi-model task
- Aria Daily Summary (a7e7a820): 6 consecutive errors — stale alert from May 25

Root cause: No platform-level ~ normalization in isolated sessions. Write tool silently fails on ~ paths. No timeout calibration against actual execution durations.

Workaround: safe-path.sh mandate in all 3 agent-write cron prompts. Timeouts bumped where possible.

Structural fix needed: OpenClaw auto-expand ~ in isolated sessions, OR reject with clear error. Audit all cron timeouts against 7-day execution history.

L-045: Agent models ignore absolute-path warnings in isolated cron sessions. 6 crons affected across 2 failure modes.

FAILURE MODE 1 - Tilde-Path (patched 2026-05-29):
- Morning Stand-Up (3c279099): tilde path, 22 days stale
- Daily Blog (a027fd60): timeout 600s + path risk, bumped to 900s
- Aria ROI (7a4d8381): tilde path in workspace-business

FAILURE MODE 2 - Timeout (folded into this ticket):
- PG Sync Check (f7668f6a): 4 consecutive 30s timeouts, too short
- Monthly Model Strategy Review (38d77d14): 300s timeout, complex task
- Aria Daily Summary (a7e7a820): 6 consecutive errors, May 25 alert

Root cause: No platform-level tilde normalization in isolated sessions. Write tool silently fails on tilde paths. No timeout calibration against actual execution durations.

Workaround: safe-path.sh mandate in all 3 agent-write cron prompts. Timeouts bumped.

Structural fix: OpenClaw auto-expand tilde in isolated sessions, OR reject with clear error. Audit all cron timeouts against 7-day execution history.
